title: Financial investment professional
overviewOfRole: Provide investment-related financial services to stakeholders specific to investment banking, investment management, or investment operations

knowledges.detail: The structure of the financial services industry, its sectors, and the role of specific organisations.
Regulation and legislation related to the handling of financial investments, including GDPR, anti-money laundering, fraud prevention, and cybersecurity frameworks.

External factors, the wider landscape, and their impact on financial services delivery, for example, government budget announcements and economic changes, microeconomics, macroeconomics, and strategic analysis frameworks such as PESTEL, SWOT, and Balanced Scorecard.
Risk and governance frameworks, how these impact their role, how to facilitate compliance, and the implications of non-compliance for their organisation.

Approaches to identifying stakeholder segments, assessing their needs, and selecting appropriate service channels for both internal and external stakeholders, applying the principles of excellent customer service and effective stakeholder relationship management.
Principles of data analysis and research, including their application to meet business and customer needs.
Data visualisation techniques, such as producing graphs, dashboards, and charts appropriate for technical and non-technical audiences.
Organisational values, including approaches to ethics, sustainability, and equity, diversity, and inclusion.
Financial asset classes and their derivatives, financial products, and financial services relevant to their role, including their features, benefits, and risks, such as green financing options.
Principles and approaches to risk management in financial investment activities.
Approaches to communication with stakeholders, including conveying complex messages to non-technical stakeholders with emotional intelligence.
Approaches to assessing wider industry demographics and the competitor landscape.
Mathematics and statistics that underpin financial models, including compound interest, cash flow models, discounting, option pricing, and valuations and credit risk.
Fundamentals of digital financial packages and applications, including the maintenance of key databases and spreadsheets.

Principles and properties behind systems, applications, and digital technology, including statistical and machine learning models, their practical applications within the organisation, and the ethical use of artificial intelligence.
Principles, tools, and techniques of workflow management, time management, and prioritisation for meeting deadlines in financial operations.
Approaches to measuring performance outcomes and productivity, including impacts on wider organisational priorities.
Principles of maintaining internal documentation to ensure a clear and identifiable audit trail of decision-making and analysis, including collaborative use of digital tools.
Principles of accounts and financial statements, including capital structures used to analyse corporate entities.

Approaches to Know Your Customer (KYC) activity, including the macro and microenvironments of each customer, their challenges and motivations, and the internal and external factors that impact those challenges, motivations, and financial decisions.
Approaches to client portfolio sales, management, and growth.
Principles of financial operations administration to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
Approaches to process mapping and efficiency strategies.

behaviours.detail: Takes responsibility for personal and professional development, such as producing and following a development plan.
Acts professionally and ethically when undertaking investment activities, demonstrating organisational values.
Is a team player, collaborating with colleagues and stakeholders.
Adapts to changing circumstances and contexts, maintaining personal resilience and learning from experience.
Creative, innovative, and enterprising when seeking solutions to problems within their remit.
Displays empathy, contributing to a diverse and inclusive culture, and uses self-awareness and emotional intelligence to respond to challenges.

skills.detail: Use emotional intelligence and interpersonal skills to build ethically sound and trusted relationships with stakeholders.
Work with stakeholders to establish their existing position, needs, and expectations, and to identify needs they may not recognise, utilising specialist expertise as required.
Use internal systems or financial services digital systems and administrative processes in compliance with organisational policies and procedures.
Identify and communicate risks to the relevant management or compliance personnel within the organisation, and follow the appropriate organisational processes to address or mitigate risk.
Select and use project management techniques applicable to the circumstances to support the delivery of projects, or parts of a project, as required.
Contribute to the development of strategic and operational plans for their organisational area.
Manage and report on progress and operational plans to stakeholders, such as senior leaders and team members, regarding their area of responsibility.
Gather, analyse, and evaluate financial information from a range of perspectives to draw sustainable, evidence-based conclusions.
Make sustainable financial investment services decisions using a structured approach.
Undertake financial investment risk management activities, such as credit risk management, market risk, operational risk management, and conduct risk management.
Plan and organise workloads, prioritise tasks, and manage time to meet key performance indicators or deadlines.
Communicate financial services concepts and present data insights in a manner appropriate to diverse audiences, adapting communication techniques accordingly.
Engage and network with stakeholders, including senior management, to build positive working relationships and deliver organisational objectives.
Keep up to date with developments in digital technology and relevant legal, regulatory, commercial, and sectoral changes, and alert others to their impacts.
Identify opportunities to drive more effective performance for own role and improve services and or processes.

Use digital tools to assist with financial investment tasks in line with cyber and data security requirements, handling data securely and safely, including backing it up in accordance with organisational policies.
Adapt and respond to new requests or unforeseen events to maintain high-quality delivery of financial tasks.
Interpret the accounts and financial statements of companies and financial institutions.
Undertake complex financial modelling, research, and analysis using appropriate tools.
Deliver complex financial or asset modelling, valuation, or pricing techniques using appropriate tools to develop solutions and recommendations.
Use trading and risk management tools under supervision.
Support the development and ongoing management of stakeholder solutions, investment strategies, and portfolios.
Generate qualitative and quantitative insights and provide bespoke client market and product information to deliver accessible and ethical information that meets client needs.
Manage financial operations processes relevant to their area.
Deliver investment operations projects and control framework execution.
Undertake team coaching to improve the performance of financial investment operations.
Undertake budgetary planning.

eQAProvider.providerName: Ofqual is the intended EQA provider
eQAProvider.contactAddress:
Ofqual
Earlsdon Park, 53-55 Butts Road, Coventry, CV1 3BH

involvedEmployers: Aon, Bank of America, Bank of New York Mellon, Black Rock, BPP, Chartered Banking Institute, Chartered Institute of Securities and Investments, DARE, Evenlode Investment , Fitch Learning, Goldman Sachs, HSBC, International Compliance Association, Investment 2020, JP Morgan, Kaplan, M and G, MAN , Nationwide Building Society, Queen Mary University London, Royal London, Standard Chartered, UBS, University of Exeter
englishAndMathsQualifications: <p>English and maths qualifications must be completed in line with the <a rel="noopener" href="https://www.gov.uk/guidance/apprenticeship-funding-rules" target="_blank">apprenticeship funding rules</a>.</p>
reviewDetails: this apprenticeship will be reviewed in accordance with our change request policy.
clusterDescription: Provide high quality advice and support to internal and external clients across a full range of financial instruments and services using specialist knowledge of instruments and services supported and areas of the business worked in

duties.dutyDetail: Identify stakeholder investment needs and risk appetite to enable the best service to be provided, such as banking solutions, investment management strategies, or trading opportunities.

Use specialised financial acumen relevant to the investment team to provide flexible and innovative financial investment solutions for stakeholders, such as clients, customers, internal colleagues, or external bodies.
Assess and monitor the impact of investment services solutions for stakeholders being accountable for the outcomes of any guidance or recommendations given.

Collaborate with internal and or external stakeholders, subject matter experts, and specialists within the investment services sector in order to build strong working relations to drive up performance and quality of service.
Provide expertise and support to internal and or external stakeholders to meet organisational service requirements and those of the wider financial services regulatory landscape.

Select, gather, evaluate, and analyse financial qualitative and quantitative data appropriate to stakeholder needs, such as feedback reports, portfolio and product performance metrics, cost reduction or cost avoidance options, efficiency gains opportunities, client or product pricing, or revenue generation.
Deliver investment services activities against individual and team performance measures, such as revenue generation, efficiency savings, or return on capital targets.
Deliver investment services activities within regulatory and compliance requirements.
Engage with stakeholders to network across the organisation to help embed a cohesive culture.

Collaborate with senior team members, for example high-value stakeholder leads or subject matter experts, to deliver investment activities such as financial product recommendations or analyses, client or asset pricing, client or firm profitability analysis, and financial or asset modelling.

Communicate complex investment and financial information to stakeholders to ensure a mutual understanding of opportunities, risks, and mitigations, such as changes to trade flows, pricing, payments, regulatory changes, and their impacts on investments and stakeholders.
Plan and undertake continuous professional development within the investment sector by maintaining current sectorial knowledge.
Contribute to identifying and rectifying areas for improvement in investment activities within the organisation.

Support clients with account planning and financial advice to aid the implementation of financial and investment solutions that meet their needs and risk appetite, such as pitch books and the delivery of investment pitches to stakeholders.
Use financial modelling and apply risk management tools to support stakeholder investment decision-making.
Use market data to develop, research, and contribute to the creation of strategic materials for teams and clients.
Prepare and assist in preparation for stakeholder interactions, for example, gaining new clients, reviewing investment portfolios, and handling complaints.

Provide ongoing management and support for client solutions and investment strategies, for example, overseeing the performance of existing investments, monitoring key metrics, implementing value-creation initiatives to optimise returns, conducting real-time monitoring of trading activity, and managing stakeholder liabilities to produce data and statistics that inform investment decision-making.

Research, analyse, and suggest options for investment strategies for stakeholder needs, for example, pricing activities in real time, conducting market analysis, ensuring profit and loss is recorded accurately, daily risk delivery or accurate recording of expenditure.
Assist stakeholders with administrative duties, such as uploading, recording, and executing trades.
Manage the administration of Investment operations activities to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and meet efficiency expectations.

Contribute to ensuring that regulatory, compliance, and commercial changes are rapidly and appropriately combined in live investment operation processes and procedures to ensure the effective functioning of the organisation.

Carry out stress tests or health checks on investment services processes and procedures to ensure that commercial outcomes are compliant and viable, for example, reconciliations, oversight of control framework performance, and identification of arising operational risks.
Contribute to ensuring that the delivery of investment customer service outcomes to stakeholders is maintained and improved in line with organisational expectations and standards.
